title: Aloo Gobi

ingredients.title: Spices
items: 1 tsp red chili powder (adjust to taste)

instruction.title: 1. Parboil the Vegetables (Optional but Restaurant-Style)
steps: Boil cauliflower florets for 2–3 minutes until just tender, then drain and set aside.
Parboil potatoes for 5 minutes; this ensures even cooking and prevents them from breaking apart in the curry.

instruction.title: 2. Temper the Spices
steps: Heat oil/ghee in a heavy-bottomed pan over medium heat.
Add cumin seeds; let them sizzle for 30 seconds.

instruction.title: 3. Sauté Aromatics
steps: Add chopped onions and sauté until golden brown for deep flavor.
Add garlic and ginger; cook for 1–2 minutes until fragrant.

instruction.title: 4. Add Tomatoes & Spices
steps: Stir in chopped tomatoes and cook until soft and oil starts to separate.
Add turmeric, chili, and coriander powders; mix well.

instruction.title: 5. Cook the Vegetables
steps: Add potatoes first, stir to coat with the spice mixture. Cook 5 minutes.
Add cauliflower florets, salt, and ¼ cup water (for a slightly saucy texture).
Cover and simmer on low heat for 10–12 minutes until potatoes are cooked and cauliflower is tender but firm.

instruction.title: 6. Finish with Flavors
steps: Sprinkle garam masala and give a gentle stir.
Adjust seasoning and add a drizzle of ghee for a rich restaurant-style finish.

instruction.title: 7. Garnish & Serve
steps: Garnish with fresh coriander and serve hot with naan, roti, or steamed basmati rice.

chefTips: Texture Balance: Parboiling prevents overcooked cauliflower and mushy potatoes.
Flavor Depth: Sauté onions until deeply golden; it adds natural sweetness and richness.
Finishing Touch: A small drizzle of ghee and fresh lemon juice elevates the aroma and taste.
Presentation: Serve on a white plate, garnish with a few coriander sprigs and thinly sliced red chili for contrast.
